constraints: repeats must be an int (tensor-valued repeats not supported)
input must be on device in TILE_LAYOUT

applicability_notes: Common uses: (1) GQA KV-head expansion — replaces torch.repeat_interleave(k, num_kv_groups, dim=1) on key/value tensors before attention; (2) batch expansion for parallel sampling (dim=0). For GQA with fp32 attention math, prefer repeat_interleave in bf16 followed by ttnn.typecast to fp32 (chain: repeat_interleave -> typecast), since the layout-bound repeat_interleave then moves 2-byte instead of 4-byte elements. PCC vs torch golden >= 0.9999.; ttnn_replacement: ttnn.repeat_interleave(input_tensor, repeats={REPEATS}, dim={DIM})

placement_observations.source: unit test: ttnn.from_torch(..., layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device) with default (DRAM interleaved) memory config

placement_observations.size_descriptor: KV tensor [batch, num_kv_heads, seq_len, head_dim], expanded along dim=1 by num_kv_groups
placement_observations.source: model call site: GQA attention, k_exp = ttnn.repeat_interleave(k_for_attn, self.num_kv_groups, dim=1); op skipped entirely when num_kv_groups == 1

placement_observations.size_descriptor: expanded KV tensor [batch, num_heads, seq_len, head_dim] in bf16, typecast to fp32 afterwards
placement_observations.source: model call site: repeat_interleave performed on bf16 tensor before ttnn.typecast(..., dtype=ttnn.float32) to halve bytes moved; intermediates deallocated immediately
